meaning: adj.粘糊糊的,(分泌)粘液的,泥泞的
examples: His feet slipped in the slimy mud.
The walls were black, cold and slimy.
Somehow this early fascination with life's prodigality, with wet, slimy or creepy-crawly things, becomes systematized and methodical.
It makes an awful first impression, slippery and slimy and simply unpleasant in your hand.
Some diesel engines sputtered and quit as their fuel filters became plugged with unknown, slimy gunk.
